data.title: Bitdeer maintains zero holdings and sold 185.7 BTC this week.
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. As of the week ending April 24, its Bitcoin mining output was 185.7 BTC, but it sold 185.7 BTC during the same period, resulting in a net increase of 0 BTC. It currently maintains a zero Bitcoin holding.

data.title: XChat Launches on App Store with Enhanced Privacy Features
data.description: XChat, a messaging application developed by X, is now available for download on the App Store. According to Foresight News, users can install the app and log in using their X account without needing a phone number or email address. The app features end-to-end encryption, ensuring that messages are visible only to the sender and recipient, with the platform unable to access them. Additionally, XChat supports self-destructing messages and allows for the transfer of any type of file.

data.title: U.S. Gold Card Visa Program Approves Single Applicant
data.description:
U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick announced during a congressional hearing that the 'Gold Card' visa program, introduced by U.S. President Donald Trump, has approved only one applicant so far. According to Odaily, the program is seen as an alternative to the traditional EB-5 investment immigration plan. Companies can pay $2 million for foreign employees to apply, with an additional 1% annual maintenance fee. This allows foreigners to pay at least $1 million to legally reside and work in the U.S., eventually providing a path to citizenship.

Howard Lutnick previously stated that within days of the program's launch, slots worth $1.3 billion had been sold, with expectations of generating $1 trillion in revenue to help alleviate the U.S. fiscal deficit. Despite only one formal approval, Lutnick mentioned that hundreds of applications are currently under review.

data.title: Only one person was approved for Trump's "gold card" visa program, a stark contrast to the expected hundreds of billions of dollars.
data.description: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated at a congressional hearing that President Trump's "Gold Card" visa program has only approved one applicant so far. This program is seen as a new alternative to the traditional EB-5 immigrant investor program, allowing companies to pay $2 million to sponsor foreign employees, along with a 1% annual maintenance fee. It allows foreigners to pay at least $1 million to legally reside and work in the U.S., with a path to eventual citizenship. Lutnick previously stated that the program sold $1.3 billion worth of visas within days of its launch and is projected to generate $1 trillion in revenue to help alleviate the U.S. fiscal deficit. Although only one person has been officially approved so far, Lutnick said hundreds more applications are currently under review. (Fortune)

data.title: DoubleZero Set to Unlock Tokens on May 2
data.description: DoubleZero (2Z) is scheduled to unlock approximately 139.7 million tokens on May 2 at 13:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is valued at around $12.02 million. The event is part of the ongoing token management strategy by the Web3 asset data platform RootData.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Engages in Extensive Bond Transactions in March
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump conducted numerous bond transactions in March, with the total amount reaching several million dollars, according to Odaily. A financial disclosure report released on Friday evening revealed 175 transactions, predominantly purchases, with only 11 sales. Most of these transactions involved bonds, though some bank stocks were also included.

The report did not provide specific details for each purchase, but many of the bond transactions were valued at over $1 million. The bonds were issued by various entities, including Nvidia, General Motors, Netflix, and Boeing. Additionally, Trump disclosed acquiring shares in Bank of America and Wells Fargo. While the exact amounts for these stock purchases were not specified, the total for these four transactions was estimated to be between $130,000 and $300,000.

data.title: Financial reports revealed that Trump made dozens of bond purchases and sales transactions in March, totaling millions of dollars.
data.description: According to a financial disclosure report, US President Trump made dozens of bond purchases and sales transactions totaling millions of dollars in March. The latest report, released Friday evening, shows 175 transactions, the vast majority of which were purchases, with only 11 sales. These transactions were almost entirely bonds, but appear to also include some bank stocks. The report did not detail every single purchase, but the amounts of the dozens of bond purchases ranged across several price ranges, with many reaching at least $1 million. These bond transactions covered numerous issuers, including Nvidia (NVDA.O), General Motors (GM.N), Netflix (NFLX.O), and Boeing (BA.N). Trump also disclosed that he appears to have purchased shares in Bank of America (BAC.N) and Wells Fargo (WFC.N). Specific purchase amounts were not detailed, but the total value of these four transactions is roughly between $130,000 and $300,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Army Sergeant Major Charged with Illegal Profits on Polymarket
data.description: U.S. Army Special Forces Command Sergeant Major Gannon Van Dyke has been charged with making over $400,000 in illegal profits on the prediction market platform Polymarket. According to NS3.AI, prosecutors claim that Van Dyke used classified military intelligence to speculate on the arrest of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. Polymarket reported the suspicious trading activity to law enforcement and has been cooperating with the investigation.

data.title: Algorand's ALGO Token Added to Japan's JVCEA Green List
data.description: Algorand Foundation announced that its ALGO token has been included in the JVCEA Green List. According to Foresight News, the Japan Virtual Currency Exchange Association (JVCEA) is regulated by the Financial Services Agency (FSA) of Japan. Cryptocurrencies on this list benefit from expedited listing processes on Japanese exchanges.

data.title: Swarms Introduces Frenzy Mode API for Automated Tokenization
data.description: Swarms has launched the Frenzy Mode API, enabling developers to autonomously tokenize Agents for automatic deployment, listing, and distribution on the Swarms marketplace. According to Foresight News, the API's key features include a 2× standard agent activation rate when Frenzy Mode is enabled, support for USDC or SOL as the bonding curve pricing unit, programmable mass deployment of multiple tokenized Agents, and automatic listing on the Swarms marketplace for immediate discoverability.

data.title: Expert Warns of Increased Kidnappings Linked to Crypto Tax Data Leaks in France
data.description: Pavel Durov has highlighted a concerning trend of kidnappings targeting cryptocurrency holders in France. According to NS3.AI, there have been 41 such incidents reported in the first three and a half months of 2026. Durov attributes these crimes to leaks in tax data, suggesting that the broader collection of government data could exacerbate these risks. He warns that increased data collection by authorities might lead to more targeted attacks on individuals involved in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Grayscale Stakes 102,400 ETH Worth $237 Million in Ethereum Mini Trust
data.description: Grayscale has staked 102,400 ETH, valued at $237 million, through its Ethereum Mini Trust. According to NS3.AI, this staking activity occurred over the past 10 hours.

data.title: The U.S. soldier who illegally profited $400,000 on Polymarket was refused an account by Kalshi.
data.description: U.S. Army Special Forces Sergeant Gannon Van Dyke has been indicted for allegedly using classified military information to codedly predict the arrest of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro on the prediction market platform Polymarket, illegally profiting over $400,000. Notably, Van Dyke attempted to open an account on the competing platform Kalshi, but was rejected due to failed identity verification and KYC checks. Polymarket stated that it proactively reported his suspicious trading activity to law enforcement and fully cooperated with the investigation. This case is considered the first major criminal case of insider trading in the prediction market sector, and has once again raised market concerns about the risks of insider trading on prediction platforms. (BusinessInsider)

data.title: California Man Sentenced for Role in Cryptocurrency Money Laundering Scheme
data.description: A 22-year-old California resident, Evan Tangeman, has been sentenced to 70 months in prison for his involvement in a money laundering operation, according to Foresight News. The case centers around a multi-state criminal group that stole over $263 million in cryptocurrency through social engineering attacks. Tangeman admitted to laundering $3.5 million, assisting in converting cryptocurrency to fiat currency, and renting luxury homes in Los Angeles and Miami for the group's extravagant spending. The illicit funds were used for lavish purchases, including luxury cars, designer watches, and exorbitant nightclub bills. Law enforcement has seized several luxury vehicles connected to the case, and nine individuals have pleaded guilty.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow Amid Market Activity
data.description:
According to Foresight News, data from SoSoValue indicates that on April 24, Eastern Time, the XRP spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $6.438 million. The inflow was solely attributed to the Bitwise XRP ETF, which saw a single-day net inflow of $6.438 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $426 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF stands at $1.095 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.23%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.291 billion.

data.title: Solana Spot ETF Experiences Significant Net Outflow
data.description: According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that on April 24, Eastern Time, Solana (SOL) spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $1.1736 million. The Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) recorded a net inflow of $255,700 for the day, bringing its historical total net inflow to $158 million. Conversely, the VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL) experienced a net outflow of $1.4293 million, with its historical total net outflow reaching $299,100. As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs stands at $883 million, with a net asset ratio of 1.77%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $1.019 billion.

data.title: Nvidia Shares Surge Over 4%, Market Cap Exceeds $5 Trillion
data.description: Nvidia shares experienced a significant rise, closing over 4% higher in U.S. trading on April 24. According to NS3.AI, this increase pushed the company's market capitalization above $5 trillion, marking the first time since late October last year that Nvidia has reached this level. A report from Stanford University highlighted that generative AI adoption stands at 53%. Additionally, the release of DeepSeek-V4 with Huawei Ascend was noted as a significant development in the AI sector.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $6.4382 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $6.4382 million yesterday (April 24th, Eastern Time). Only the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP) saw net inflows yesterday, with a single-day net inflow of $6.4382 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $426 million. As of press time, the XRP spot ETF has a total net asset value of $1.095 billion, an XRP net asset ratio of 1.23%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.291 billion.

data.title: The US Solomon Islands (SOL) spot ETF saw a total net outflow of $1.1736 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (April 24th, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of SOL spot ETFs was $1.1736 million. Among them, the Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) saw a net inflow of $255,700, bringing its historical total net inflow to $158 million. The VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL) experienced a net outflow of $1.4293 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $299,100. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs was $883 million, with an equity ratio of 1.77%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.019 billion.
